symptoms: Diamond-shaped lesions on leaves with grey/white centres and brown borders. Neck blast causes panicle breakage.
conditions_that_trigger: High humidity >85%, temperature 25-28°C, excessive nitrogen fertilizer, dense planting
prevention: Use resistant varieties (MR220 CL2), balanced fertilizer, proper spacing

symptoms: Water-soaked lesions starting from leaf tips, turning yellow then white. Leaves dry out completely.
conditions_that_trigger: Warm temperatures 25-34°C, high humidity, wounds from wind or insects, flooding
treatment_steps: No effective chemical treatment once established
Remove and destroy severely infected plants
prevention: Plant resistant varieties, avoid excess nitrogen, balanced water management

severity_levels.moderate: 20-40% leaf area affected, kresek (wilting) in seedlings

symptoms: Oval/irregular lesions on leaf sheaths near water level. Lesions are greenish-grey with brown margins.
conditions_that_trigger: High temperature >28°C, high relative humidity, dense canopy, excessive nitrogen
prevention: Wide spacing, balanced fertilizer, avoid waterlogging

severity_levels.severe: Flag leaf infected, panicle emergence affected

symptoms: Sunken circular lesions on ripe fruits with dark concentric rings. Fruits rot and mummify. Can also appear on leaves and stems.
conditions_that_trigger: Rain splashing, high humidity >80%, temperature 27-32°C, wounded fruits
treatment_steps: Remove and destroy infected fruits immediately
Apply Mancozeb (Dithane M-45) at 2g/L every 7 days
Spray Propineb alternating with Copper hydroxide (Kocide)
Harvest fruits at breaker stage before full ripening
prevention: Use disease-free seeds, crop rotation, avoid overhead irrigation, good drainage

symptoms: Circular brown spots with grey centres on leaves. Spots may merge causing leaf drop. Reduced fruit set.
conditions_that_trigger: High humidity 75%+, warm temperatures, poor air circulation, rain splashing
prevention: Adequate spacing, avoid overhead watering, crop rotation

symptoms: Sudden wilting of entire plant despite adequate water. Lower leaves yellow first. Brown discolouration of vascular tissue when stem is cut.
conditions_that_trigger: Waterlogged soil, temperature >30°C, acidic soil pH <5.5, contaminated tools
treatment_steps: No chemical cure — remove and destroy infected plants immediately
Apply lime to raise soil pH above 6.0
Do not replant solanaceous crops for 2-3 seasons
prevention: Use resistant varieties, raised beds, proper drainage, tool sterilization

severity_levels.severe: Entire plot infected, total crop loss

symptoms: Upward curling of leaves, leaf size reduction, stunted growth, flower drop. Transmitted by whitefly.
conditions_that_trigger: High whitefly population, dry season, proximity to infected plants
prevention: Whitefly management, reflective mulch, insect-proof nursery

symptoms: White blister-like pustules on undersides of leaves. Yellow spots on upper leaf surface corresponding to pustules.
conditions_that_trigger: Cool temperatures 15-25°C, high humidity, morning dew, dense planting
prevention: Proper spacing, morning harvest to reduce moisture, crop rotation

symptoms: Brown discolouration at stem base, wilting, yellowing leaves from bottom up. Plants collapse at soil line.
conditions_that_trigger: Waterlogged conditions, high temperature, contaminated soil, root damage
treatment_steps: Remove infected plants with surrounding soil
Avoid replanting in same spot for 1 season
prevention: Clean planting material, raised beds, crop rotation

symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of lower leaves, splitting of pseudostem base, reddish-brown discolouration of vascular tissue.
conditions_that_trigger: Contaminated soil, waterlogging, acidic soil, movement of infected planting material
treatment_steps: NO CURE — destroy infected plants including roots
Quarantine area — do not replant banana for 5+ years
prevention: Use tissue culture seedlings, raised beds, strict hygiene, TR4-resistant varieties

severity_levels.severe: Total mat destruction, spreads to neighbours

symptoms: Dark brown streaks on leaves expanding to large necrotic areas. Premature leaf death reduces fruit size and quality.
conditions_that_trigger: High rainfall, humidity >80%, temperature 25-35°C, poor leaf management
treatment_steps: Remove infected leaves below the bunch
Rotate fungicide groups to prevent resistance
prevention: Remove dead leaves, adequate spacing, resistant varieties

symptoms: Long cigar-shaped greyish-green lesions on leaves, 2.5-15cm long. Start from lower leaves and move upward.
conditions_that_trigger: Moderate temperature 18-27°C, heavy dew, cloudy weather, continuous corn planting
treatment_steps: Spray Propiconazole if lesions reach ear leaf
prevention: Resistant hybrids, crop rotation, balanced fertilizer

severity_levels.moderate: Lesions on upper leaves, 30% leaf area
severity_levels.severe: All leaves blighted before grain fill

symptoms: Ragged holes in leaves with visible frass (sawdust-like excrement). Larvae feed in whorl, destroying growing point. Silk clipping at ear stage.
conditions_that_trigger: Dry conditions, new plantings adjacent to mature corn, lack of natural enemies
treatment_steps: Spray Emamectin Benzoate for larger larvae
Apply Bacillus thuringiensis (Dipel) for early instar
prevention: Early planting, trap crops, release Trichogramma wasps, pheromone traps

symptoms: Raised corky lesions on stems and leaf petioles. Leaves distort and curl. Stunted vine growth.
conditions_that_trigger: High humidity, warm temperature, rain splashing, infected planting material
prevention: Certified clean cuttings, crop rotation, avoid overhead irrigation

severity_levels.moderate: Widespread stem scab, some vine stunting

symptoms: Entry holes in tubers with frass. Internal tunnelling makes tubers bitter and inedible. Adults feed on vine stems.
conditions_that_trigger: Dry soil conditions, cracked soil exposing tubers, long growing season
treatment_steps: Apply Chlorpyrifos (Lorsban) soil drench at planting
Hill up soil around tubers to prevent exposure
prevention: Short duration varieties, proper hilling, irrigation to prevent soil cracking, crop rotation

symptoms: Round entry holes on fruits, internal feeding damage, frass visible. Larvae bore into immature and mature fruits.
conditions_that_trigger: Flowering and fruiting stage, dry weather, lack of natural enemies
treatment_steps: Apply Emamectin Benzoate at first sign
prevention: Pheromone traps, Trichogramma releases, neem-based sprays, intercropping with marigold

symptoms: Circular patches of dried plants (hopper burn). Honeydew on leaves promotes sooty mould. Stunted tillers.
conditions_that_trigger: Excessive nitrogen, dense planting, continuous flooding, lack of natural enemies
treatment_steps: Spray Imidacloprid (Confidor) at base of plants
Alternate wetting and drying to reduce habitat
prevention: Moderate nitrogen, resistant varieties, light traps, intermittent irrigation

symptoms: Leaves become narrow, upright, and bunched at top. Dark green streaks on petioles (Morse code pattern). Stunted growth, no fruit.
conditions_that_trigger: Banana aphid (Pentalonia nigronervosa) transmission, infected planting material
treatment_steps: NO CURE — uproot and destroy infected plants
Kill aphids with Imidacloprid before uprooting
Do not use suckers from infected mats
prevention: Certified tissue culture seedlings, aphid control, surveillance, quarantine

severity_levels.severe: Plant never fruits, virus spreads to neighbours

symptoms: Chlorotic striping on leaves, white/grey downy growth on underside. Stunted plants with tassel and ear malformation.
conditions_that_trigger: Cool nights, heavy dew, humidity >90%, early infection of seedlings
treatment_steps: Apply Metalaxyl seed treatment before planting
Remove infected plants to prevent spore spread
prevention: Resistant varieties, seed treatment, avoid planting in heavily infected areas
